name: eval-conversation
description: 2-speaker in-person script. Turn order IS the diarization ground truth; the proper nouns (Recato, Priya, Sunnyvale, cortado, Arjun) are the vocab-biasing payoff.

turns.speaker: A
turns.text: Hey, did you finish the report for Recato?

turns.speaker: B
turns.text: Almost. I just need Priya to review the last part.

turns.speaker: A
turns.text: Cool. Is the meeting still at three?

turns.speaker: B
turns.text: Yeah, three at the Sunnyvale office. Are you driving?

turns.speaker: A
turns.text: I'll take the train. Want me to grab coffee?

turns.speaker: B
turns.text: Please — a cortado for me, thanks.

turns.speaker: A
turns.text: Got it. Did Arjun send the slides yet?

turns.speaker: B
turns.text: He sent them this morning. They look good.

turns.speaker: A
turns.text: Nice. Let's wrap up by four.

turns.speaker: B
turns.text: Sounds good. See you at three.
